what_it_does: This file is a guided-review finding that traces the existing singular generate-to-approve-to-dispatch-to-git loop, verifies that the consent, dispatch, git-commit, and revert machinery is real for one intent, and reports that batch compose/approve/dispatch, a 'generate' orchestrator, and automated mockup-edit routing do not exist.
observations: The single-intent wire exists end-to-end: surface_intent_at, /api/intent-at, /api/resolve, dispatch_decision, launch via claude -p, git commit, and manual revert.
Empty or orphan ui:// addresses produce empty scope and DENY-ALL via resolve_scope.
The default posture is safe-by-default plan mode; acceptEdits and wire_armed are opt-in.
No batch path exists: every producer, approve, and dispatch caller is singular; drive_dispatchable only loops over independently approved verdicts.
There is no 'generate' compose-step or accumulation buffer; mintBuildIntent mints one intent per click.
Mockup-vs-live is not a deliberate router: unbuilt mockups get empty scope and are unbuildable, while mockup feedback is a retired manual loop.
approve_reach / blast_radius is a second, already-built consent dimension for scope widening.
Revert is manual and post-commit via /api/revert; there is no auto-revert after commit.
standouts: The singular consent/dispatch/git spine is already solid; what is missing is batch composition and UX, not governance.
Mockup-edit dispatch does not exist as an automated path; the mockup/live distinction is an emergent side-effect of empty scope.
approve_reach is a live second consent dimension that needs a conscious keep-or-cut decision.
conventions: Uses evidence tags: Observed, Inferred, Anchor-idea, External.
Numbered sections with explicit verdicts and 'net-new' vs 'real' classification.
Distinguishes the existing singular wire from the missing batch and mockup paths.
concerns: Some language ('airtight', 'unkillable') is stronger than a static grep finding warrants.
The inferred 'plausible shape' and proposed router are speculative design, not observed fact.
Tone is occasionally argumentative toward an unseen 'anchor' document, which may reduce neutrality for a ledger entry.
notes: A code-review finding arguing that the single-intent build loop is real and simple, but batch generate/compose/approve and mockup-edit routing are missing net-new work.
questions: What is the exact 'anchor' document or claim being responded to?
What UI sequence is meant by 'marked-up addresses' — is it a wizard, a checklist, or a chat transcript?
Is 'generate' intended as one button, a composed review item, or a background orchestrator?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches its stated purpose, but shifts emphasis from 'the wire exists end-to-end' to 'only the singular wire exists; batch and mockup paths are missing.'
apparent_themes: Reuse the existing governance and git spine rather than rebuild it.
Keep consent simple — one approve per unit of work.
The hard part is live guided composition and UX, not wiring.
Operator authority and manual post-commit revert.
intention_signals: It seeks to validate the anchor's thesis that simple consent is sufficient while correcting overclaim: the single-intent wire is already built, but the batch compose/one-approve experience and mockup routing remain to be constructed on top of it.
novelty: It reframes the missing 'generate' step as a composition/UX problem rather than a consent/governance problem, and shows that mockup-vs-live routing is emergent from scope resolution rather than an explicit design router.
contribution: A traced, evidence-tagged audit of the singular build-intent wire plus an explicit inventory of net-new components (accumulation buffer, batch compose, batch approve, mockup-edit dispatch) that a fused system would need to implement for sequence-based generation.
summary_for_embedding: Guided-review finding tracing the singular build-intent wire from surface_intent_at through dispatch, claude -p launch, git commit, and manual revert, and reporting that batch compose/one-approve, a generate orchestrator, and automated mockup-edit dispatch are absent.
intention_for_embedding: To verify the anchor's claim that the generate-to-git wire already exists and to surface which parts are real versus net-new composition and routing work.
